answer:
The function is defined when the denominator is nonzero and the radicand is positive, so we need \(x^2 - 5x + 6 > 0\).  
Factoring gives \((x-2)(x-3) > 0\), which holds for \(x < 2\) or \(x > 3\).  

Thus, the domain is \((-\infty, 2) \cup (3, \infty)\).

\boxed{(-\infty, 2) \cup (3, \infty)}
